texto: Love is RealWhat would we have for the world? Would we have it lost in darkness and hate or would we have it in the light of love? What we extend is what we see, extend love and it will grow to encompass all things. Love is real, hate, fear is not…repeat that over and over again...Love is real, I choose to see a world forgiven, I choose to see my brother as I see myself, loved beyond measure whole and holy, blessed…Look past the nightmare your brother believes real and see them for who they are, Holy Children of God pretending to be that which they are not.Love and Light
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Sharon Kay Casey que provoca vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. Ao questionar “Love is RealWhat would we have for the world? Would we have it lost in darkness and hate or would…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre fe, light-and-darkness, love, com foco em reflexao-noturna e boa-noite.

texto: All that we see around us is not what it appears…As is the second coming not what we thought it would be. It is the knowledge that the spirit of God lives in all things and all things are joined together in the light of Love who is God. Thus accepting the truth of who you are
